title: Resume Builder
description: Build a civilian-ready, ATS-optimized resume from your military experience.

intake.label: Full name
What civilian job or role are you targeting?
Military branch
MOS / Rating / AFSC (job code)
Years of service
Top 3-5 skills or accomplishments to highlight
Education (degrees, certifications, schools)

title: LinkedIn Profile Builder
description: Create a compelling LinkedIn headline, summary, and experience section translated for civilian audiences.

intake.label: Full name
What industry or role are you pursuing?
Military branch
MOS / Rating / AFSC
Key accomplishments or leadership experiences
How do you want to be perceived? (e.g., leader, problem-solver, technical expert)

title: General Power of Attorney
description: Generate a general power of attorney document designating someone to act on your behalf.
legal_disclaimer: This template provides a starting framework only. It is NOT legal advice. Have an attorney review before signing.

intake.label: What state do you live in?
Your full legal name (the principal)
Full legal name of the person you're granting power to (the agent)
Relationship to agent (spouse, parent, sibling, friend, etc.)
What powers are you granting? (financial, property, legal, all)
When should this take effect?

title: Last Will and Testament
description: Create a basic will outlining how your assets and responsibilities should be handled.
legal_disclaimer: This template provides a starting framework only. It is NOT legal advice. State laws vary. Have an attorney review before signing.

intake.label: What state do you live in?
Your full legal name
Marital status
Names and ages of children (if any)
Who should be your executor (the person who carries out your will)?
Who should receive your assets? List names and what each should receive.
If you have minor children, who should be their guardian?

title: Living Will (Advance Directive)
description: Document your healthcare wishes in case you become unable to communicate them yourself.
upload_suggestions: prior living will or advance directive
legal_disclaimer: This template provides a starting framework only. It is NOT legal advice. State requirements vary. Have an attorney or healthcare provider review.

intake.label: What state do you live in?
Your full legal name
If you are terminally ill or permanently unconscious, do you want life-sustaining treatment?
options: No, I want comfort care only
I want treatment tried for a limited time, then comfort care

intake.label: Do you want artificial nutrition and hydration (feeding tube/IV)?
Pain management preferences
options: Keep me comfortable even if it may hasten death
I want to remain as alert as possible

intake.label: Any other specific medical wishes?

title: VA Claim Personal Statement
description: Write a structured personal statement to support your VA disability claim.

intake.label: Your full name
Military branch
What condition(s) are you claiming?
Describe the in-service event, injury, or exposure that caused or worsened this condition
How does this condition affect your daily life and work today?
What treatment have you received (military and civilian)?

legal_disclaimer: This statement is a template to help organize your thoughts. It is NOT legal advice. Consider working with a VA-accredited claims agent or Veterans Service Organization (VSO) for best results.

title: Debt Hardship Letter
description: Write a professional hardship letter to creditors requesting modified payment terms or debt relief.

intake.label: Your full name
Who is the creditor or debt collector?
Account number (optional — helps identify your account)
Approximate amount owed
What caused the hardship? (medical emergency, job loss, disability, transition, etc.)
What are you asking for? (lower payments, settlement, deferment, forgiveness)
Are you a veteran or active service member?

legal_disclaimer: This letter is a template starting point. Results vary by creditor. Consider consulting a nonprofit credit counselor (NFCC.org) or legal aid for your situation.

title: Durable Power of Attorney
description: Create a durable POA that remains valid even if you become incapacitated.
legal_disclaimer: This template provides a starting framework only. It is NOT legal advice. Have an attorney review before signing.

intake.label: What state do you live in?
Your full legal name (the principal)
Full legal name of your agent (attorney-in-fact)
Relationship to agent
Successor agent (backup if primary agent cannot serve)
What powers are you granting? (financial, property, legal, healthcare, all)
Any limitations or restrictions on the agent's powers?

title: Medical Power of Attorney
description: Designate someone to make healthcare decisions on your behalf if you cannot.
legal_disclaimer: This template provides a starting framework only. It is NOT legal advice. Have an attorney or healthcare provider review.

intake.label: What state do you live in?
Your full legal name
Full legal name of your healthcare agent
Relationship to agent
Agent's phone number
Alternate agent (backup if primary cannot serve)
Any specific medical instructions or preferences for your agent?
Should your agent have authority over mental health treatment decisions?

title: HIPAA Authorization Form
description: Authorize specific individuals or organizations to access your protected health information.

intake.label: Your full legal name (the patient)
Date of birth
Who are you authorizing to receive your health information? (name and relationship)
Healthcare provider or facility that holds your records
What type of information can be released?
Purpose of the release (legal, personal, VA claim, insurance, etc.)
When should this authorization expire?
Additional notes or restrictions

legal_disclaimer: This is a template only and does NOT constitute legal authorization. Consult a healthcare provider or attorney to ensure compliance with your state HIPAA requirements.

title: Emergency Contact & Family Care Plan
description: Create a comprehensive emergency contact sheet and family care plan for deployment or crisis situations.

intake.label: Your full name
Names and ages of dependents (children, elderly parents, pets, etc.)
Primary emergency contact (name, phone, relationship)
Secondary emergency contact (name, phone, relationship)
Designated caregiver for dependents if you are unavailable (name, phone)
Important medical info (allergies, medications, conditions for you or dependents)
Key financial info (bank name, insurance provider — no account numbers)
Special instructions (school pickup, pet care, religious preferences, etc.)

legal_disclaimer: This plan is for personal preparedness purposes. Review with your chain of command or an attorney if it will be used for official military family care plan requirements.

title: VA Appeal Letter
description: Draft a structured appeal letter for a denied VA disability claim.

intake.label: Your full name
Military branch
VA claim or file number
What condition(s) were denied?
What reason did the VA give for the denial? (copy from denial letter if possible)
Date of the denial decision
What new or additional evidence do you have since the denial?
Which appeal pathway are you choosing?

legal_disclaimer: This letter is a template starting point. VA appeals have strict deadlines and requirements. Strongly consider working with a VA-accredited attorney or VSO representative.

title: Records Request Letter
description: Generate a formal request letter for military, medical, or government records.

intake.label: Your full legal name (as it appears on military records)
Military branch
Approximate dates of service (e.g., 2005-2012)
Last 4 of SSN (for record matching — optional)
What type of records are you requesting?
Which agency or facility holds these records?
Why do you need these records? (VA claim, employment, personal, legal)
Any additional identifying information or special instructions

legal_disclaimer: This is a template request. Processing times vary. For urgent needs, contact the National Personnel Records Center directly at 314-801-0800.

title: Federal Resume (USAJobs)
description: Build a detailed federal resume formatted for USAJobs applications.

intake.label: Full name
Mailing address (city, state, zip)
Email address
Phone number
U.S. citizenship status
Veterans' preference claim
Military branch
MOS / Rating / AFSC
Highest rank held
Dates of service (start — end)
Average hours per week during service
Key duties, accomplishments, and responsibilities (be detailed — federal resumes are long)
Job title and announcement number you're applying for (if known)
Education (degrees, schools, dates, GPA if 3.0+)
Certifications, licenses, and training
Security clearance (type and status)

title: Interview Prep Script (STAR Method)
description: Prepare structured interview responses using the STAR method tailored to your military experience.

intake.label: Full name
What role are you interviewing for?
Military branch
MOS / Rating / AFSC
Describe a time you led a team or project (what happened, what you did, what resulted)
Describe a major challenge you overcame during service
Describe a time you worked with a difficult team or resolved a conflict
Any other strengths or experiences you want to highlight?

title: Credit Dispute Letter
description: Draft a formal dispute letter to credit bureaus for inaccurate items on your credit report.

intake.label: Your full legal name
Your current mailing address
Which credit bureau are you disputing with?
Name of the creditor or account being disputed
Account number (partial is fine)
Why is this item inaccurate?
options: Account paid but still showing balance

intake.label: Explain the inaccuracy in detail
Are you a veteran or active service member?

legal_disclaimer: This letter follows FCRA dispute guidelines but is NOT legal advice. If disputes are complex, consider consulting a consumer rights attorney.

title: Budget / Financial Recovery Plan
description: Create a structured monthly budget and financial recovery roadmap.

intake.label: Your name
Total monthly income (take-home pay, VA disability, etc.)
List your income sources (employment, VA disability %, GI Bill, etc.)
Monthly housing cost (rent/mortgage + utilities)
Monthly transportation cost (car payment, insurance, gas)
Monthly food/groceries cost
List your debts (creditor name, balance, minimum payment for each)
Other monthly expenses (phone, insurance, childcare, subscriptions, etc.)
What is your primary financial goal?
options: Create a budget I can stick to

legal_disclaimer: This budget plan is for educational purposes. Consult a certified financial counselor for personalized financial advice. Free counseling is available through Military OneSource.

title: Nexus Letter Prep Template
description: Prepare the supporting information your doctor needs to write a nexus letter connecting your condition to service.

intake.label: Your full name
Military branch
Dates of service
What condition do you need the nexus letter for?
What in-service event, injury, or exposure caused or contributed to this condition?
When did symptoms first appear? (during service, shortly after, years later)
Current diagnosis (if you have one) and treating doctor's name
What treatment are you currently receiving?
Have you filed a VA claim for this condition before? If denied, what was the reason?

legal_disclaimer: This prep sheet helps organize information for your medical provider. The final nexus letter MUST be written and signed by a qualified medical professional.

title: Benefits Eligibility Summary
description: Generate a personalized summary of federal and state benefits you may be eligible for.

intake.label: Your full name
What state do you live in?
Military branch
Service era
Discharge type
Current VA disability rating (if any)
Total years of service
Marital status
Number of dependents (children, spouse, parents)
Have you used GI Bill benefits?
Current employment status

legal_disclaimer: This summary is generated from the information you provided and general eligibility rules. Actual eligibility is determined by the VA. Verify with VA.gov or a VSO.

title: Military to Civilian Skills Translator
description: Translate your military job code, training, and experience into civilian-friendly language.

intake.label: Full name
Military branch
MOS / Rating / AFSC (job code)
Official military job title
Highest rank held
Describe your primary duties and responsibilities
Equipment, systems, or technology you used
Number of people you supervised or led
What civilian industry or role are you interested in? (or 'not sure')

title: Salary Negotiation Script
description: Build a confident salary negotiation script based on your experience and market data.

intake.label: Full name
Job title you're negotiating for
Company name
Salary they offered (or expected range)
What salary do you want?
Total years of relevant experience (military + civilian)
What unique value do you bring? (clearance, specialized training, leadership, certifications)
Do you have competing offers or alternative options?
options: No — this is my only option

intake.label: Are there non-salary items you'd also negotiate? (signing bonus, remote work, PTO, relocation, etc.)

title: VA Loan Readiness Checklist
description: Generate a personalized checklist of everything you need to apply for a VA home loan.

intake.label: Your full name
What state are you buying in?
Current service status
Do you have your Certificate of Eligibility (COE)?
Approximate credit score range
Total monthly gross income
Total monthly debt payments (car, credit cards, loans, etc.)
Approximate savings available for closing costs
Is this your first home purchase?
options: No — first time using VA loan
No — have used VA loan before

intake.label: What type of property are you looking at?

legal_disclaimer: This checklist is for preparation purposes only. VA loan requirements may change. Work with a VA-approved lender for current guidelines.

title: Rental Application Packet
description: Prepare a complete rental application packet including cover letter and reference sheet.

intake.label: Your full name
Phone number
Email address
Monthly gross income
Income sources (employment, VA disability, GI Bill, etc.)
Current address
Reason for moving (PCS, transition, lease ending, etc.)
Veteran or active duty?
Do you have pets? (type and number)
Reference 1 (name, phone, relationship — e.g., former landlord, supervisor)
Reference 2 (name, phone, relationship)
Anything else you'd like the landlord to know?

legal_disclaimer: This packet helps organize your application materials. Landlord requirements vary. This does NOT guarantee application approval.

title: Personal Emergency Action Plan
description: Create a comprehensive personal emergency preparedness plan for you and your family.

intake.label: Your full name
How many people in your household?
Household members (names, ages, any special needs)
Home address
What emergencies are you most concerned about?
Emergency contact 1 (name, phone, relationship)
Emergency contact 2 (name, phone, relationship)
Family meeting point if you need to evacuate (address or landmark)
Any critical medical needs in your household? (medications, equipment, conditions)
Pets that need to be included in the plan (type and number)

legal_disclaimer: This plan is for personal preparedness purposes only. Verify all contact information and procedures with relevant authorities.
